title: uragoga apoda
side_effects: Uragoga apoda can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, nausea, and vomiting in some individuals. Additionally, it may cause dizziness, headaches, and rapid heartbeat due to the plant's high concentration of toxic compounds. In extreme cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can lead to liver or kidney damage, as well as neurological issues like numbness and tremors. If not handled carefully, uragoga apoda can also cause skin irritation, rashes, and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals.
